---
title: "Citydeal_data_analysis"
author: "Sjoerd Gremmen"
date: "2024-12-02"
output: html_document
---

# Data preparation

The input of this analysis should be an ASV/OTU table, metadata. The OTU table I use is obtained using DADA2 before this. (In future, hopefully see other markdown file). Besides that ASV/OTU file a taxonomy file might be loaded in. This is the output from Unite for ITS and Silva for 16s.

We need dplyr for some filtering

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(RVAideMemoire)
library("ANCOMBC")
library("microbiome")
library(phyloseq)
library(pheatmap)
library(tidyverse)
library(data.table)
library(vegan)
library(microeco)
library(scales)
library(devtools)
#install_github("mahendra-mariadassou/phyloseq-extended", ref = "dev", force=TRUE)
library("phyloseq.extended")
```

```{r}
radial_theta <- function(x) {
    x <- as(x, "matrix")
    theta <- atan2((x[, 2] - mean(x[, 2])), (x[, 1] - mean(x[, 1])))
    names(theta) <- rownames(x)
    theta
}
```

```{r}
datafolder <- setwd() ## setwd
```

## Loading ASV/OTU file

First we read the ASV/OTU table from your file into this

```{r}
#First import ASV/OTU_Table from either ITS or 16S
otu_table=read.csv(paste(datafolder, "/ITS_ASV_table.csv"),row.names=1, header = T, sep=";")
# otu_table=read.csv(paste(datafolder, "/ASV_16S_table.csv"),row.names=1, header = T, sep=";")
head(otu_table)
otu_table=as.matrix(otu_table)
mean(colSums(otu_table))
nrow(otu_table)

# Taxonomy file (output unite/silva)
taxonomy=read.csv(paste(datafolder, "/taxa_list"), row.names=1, header = T, sep =",")
taxonomy$sequence <- NULL
replace(taxonomy, taxonomy == " ", NA) -> tax1
taxonomy1=as.matrix(tax1)

## Metadata
metadata=read.csv(paste(datafolder, "/metadata"), row.names=1)
metadata
```

The metadata has to be filtered

### Filtering metadata

```{r}
# In my case the metadata has multiple experiments in there. Here you could filter them on column
  metadata <- metadata[metadata$experiment=="CityDeal",]
  metadata <- metadata[!grepl("^Lin", rownames(metadata)),]
  metadata <- metadata[!grepl("^Gras", rownames(metadata)),]
  metadata <- metadata[rownames(metadata) != "T1A2B.C", ]
  metadata <- metadata[rownames(metadata) != "T1A2B.B", ]
  rownames(metadata)[rownames(metadata) == "T1A2B.A"] <- "T1A2B"
# The filtering is now complete. Some of the metadata has to be translated though
metadata$topology <- gsub("Weg_bomen", "Street_with_trees", metadata$topology)
metadata$topology <- gsub("Stoep_bomen", "Sidewalk_with_trees", metadata$topology)
metadata$topology <- gsub("Stoep_geenbomen", "Sidewalk_without_trees", metadata$topology)
metadata$topology <- gsub("Weg_geenbomen", "Street_without_trees", metadata$topology)
metadata$topology <- gsub("Boomspiegel", "Treepit", metadata$topology)
```

If necessary we can save the file:

```{r}
#Now we have completed the filtering, I'm going to save the filtered metadata
write.csv(metadata, paste(datafolder, "/metadata_filtered.csv"))
```

After filtering the metadata, I would like to do the same with the ASV table

### Filtering ASV table

```{r}
colnames(otu_table)[colnames(otu_table) == "T1A2B.A"] <- "T1A2B"
# Extract sample names from metadata (rownames)
meta_samples <- rownames(metadata)

# Extract sample names from OTU table (colnames)
otu_samples <- colnames(otu_table)

# Find samples that are in both metadata and OTU table
common_samples <- intersect(meta_samples, otu_samples)

# Filter the OTU table to keep only common samples
otu_table <- otu_table[, common_samples]

# Optional: Check dimensions of the filtered OTU table
dim(otu_table)
## Remove ASVs with zeroes
table_nozeroes <- otu_table %>% filter(rowSums(otu_table)>0)
dim(table_nozeroes)
mean(colSums(table_nozeroes))
```

I like to remove unnecessary data from my workspace, because I'm working on limited RAM

### Remove unnecessary data

```{r}
rm(tax1)
rm(taxonomy)
rm(common_samples)
rm(meta_samples)
rm(otu_samples)
```

## Phyloseq element

Now we have loaded in all data, we are going to put everything into a phyloseq element.

For this we first need to load the package phyloseq.

### Packages

```{r}
if (!require("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE)){
    install.packages("BiocManager")}
library("BiocManager")
BiocManager::install("phyloseq")
## some extra functions for phyloseq
scripts <- c("graphical_methods.R", "tree_methods.R", "plot_merged_trees.R", "specificity_methods.R", "ternary_plot.R",
             "richness.R", "edgePCA.R", "copy_number_correction.R", "import_frogs.R", "prevalence.R", "compute_niche.R")
urls <- paste0("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mahendra-mariadassou/phyloseq-extended/master/R/", scripts)
for (url in urls) { source(url) }


#BiocManager::install("phyloseq")

library("microbiome")
```

### Preparing the phyloseq element

```{r}
OTU=otu_table(otu_table, taxa_are_rows=TRUE)
TAX=tax_table(taxonomy1)
head(TAX)
META=sample_data(metadata)
metadata

# For 16s a tree is available as well
#tre_fpath = "rooted_tree.nwk"
#TRE <- ape::read.tree(tre_fpath)
```

Remove the AS

### Check loaded data

```{r}
#sample_names(OTU)
#sample_names(META)
all(sample_names(OTU) == sample_names(META))
# Should be TRUE
```

### Making the phyloseq element

```{r}
physeq=phyloseq(OTU, TAX, META)
physeq
```

remove non fungal ASVs and perhaps other contamination

```{r}
## For ITS: Filter out non fungal ASVs
physeq_fungal_only <- subset_taxa(physeq, Kingdom == "Fungi")
physeq_fungal_only
table_nozeroes <- as.data.frame(physeq_fungal_only@otu_table) %>% filter(rowSums(as.data.frame(physeq_fungal_only@otu_table))>0)
dim(table_nozeroes)
mean(colSums(table_nozeroes))
```

```         
phyloseq-class experiment-level object otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 43387 taxa and 465 samples ] sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 465 samples by 12 sample variables ] tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 43387 taxa by 8 taxonomic ranks ]
```

remove stuff to make some space

```{r}
rm(list=setdiff(ls(),"physeq_fungal_only"))
```

# Rarefaction

## Check sequencing depth (Hist)

```{r}
# <- physeq_fungal_only
#rm(physeq_fungal_only)
df <- data.frame(Sample_Sums = sample_sums(physeq))

# Create the histogram
ggplot(df, aes(x = Sample_Sums)) + 
  geom_histogram(color = "black", fill = "indianred", binwidth = 10000) +
  ggtitle("Distribution of sample sequencing depth") + 
  xlab("Read counts") +
  ylab("Number of samples") +
  theme(axis.title.y = element_blank())+
  scale_x_continuous(labels = comma)
```

Based on the histogram, we can see that a lot of samples have many many reads. Earlier, I did a 10.000 cutoff, but it would kind of make sense to go for 30.000 instead. So lets try.

## Rarefaction

```{r}
sequencing_depth <- as.data.frame(sample_sums(physeq))
write.csv(df, (paste(datafolder, "/output/sequencing_depth_raw.csv"))
physeq <- subset_samples(physeq_fungal_only, sample_sums(physeq_fungal_only)>=10000)
# rarefy your dataset to lowest sequencing length or a custom number, 711 should be kept constant:
phy_rare <- rarefy_even_depth(physeq,rngseed = 711, replace = F, trimOTUs = TRUE, 	verbose = F)
phy_rare
colSums(phy_rare@otu_table)
## We did lose 12 samples in this process. 
# Sample names before rarefaction
lost_samples <- setdiff(colnames(table_nozeroes), sample_names(phy_rare@otu_table))
lost_samples
```

```         
phyloseq-class experiment-level object otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 23271 taxa and 439 samples ] sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 439 samples by 12 sample variables ] tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 23271 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]
```

### Clean internal data

```{r}
rm(physeq)
rm(df)
```

Check if rarefaction is deep enough

```{r}
ggrare(phy_rare, step = 100, color = "city", se = F)
sample_names(phy_rare)
sample_sums(phy_rare)
```

Add group to metadata

```{r}
phy_rare@sam_data$group <- paste(phy_rare@sam_data$city, phy_rare@sam_data$topology, sep = "_")
```

And ofcourse it's nice to save the rarefied data

```{r}
write.csv(phy_rare@otu_table,(paste(datafolder, "/ASV_ITS_rarefied11912.csv")))
META<- as.matrix(phy_rare@sam_data)
write.csv(META,(paste(datafolder, "/metadata_ITS_rarefied11912.csv")))

rm(META)
```

# Visualization of ASV table

## Bar graphs

### Total abundance on phylogenetic level

Ofcourse Order can be changed to any phylogenetic level

```{r}
phy_rare_typology <- merge_samples(phy_rare, "topology")
plot_bar(phy_rare_typology, fill = "Order") + 
  geom_bar(aes(color = Order, fill = Order), stat = "identity", position = "stack") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

```

```{r}
# proportional
ASV_ITS_Phylprop <- merge_samples(phy_rare, "topology")
ASV_ITS_Phylprop <- transform_sample_counts(ASV_ITS_Phylprop, function(x) x/sum(x)*100)
ASV_ITS_Phylprop <- tax_glom(ASV_ITS_Phylprop, taxrank = "Phylum", NArm = FALSE)

plot_bar(ASV_ITS_Phylprop, fill = "PhyClum")


ggsave(paste(datafolder, "/Community_type/ASV_ITS_BarClassProp_city.png"), device = png, width = 3000, height = 1080, units = "px", dpi = 144)
```

### 16S

```{r}
dim(physeq_filtered_16s@otu_table)
#[1] 158303    465
table_nozeroes <- as.data.frame(physeq_filtered_16s@otu_table) %>% filter(rowSums(as.data.frame(physeq_filtered_16s@otu_table))>0)
dim(table_nozeroes)
mean(colSums(table_nozeroes))
```

```{r}
# proportional
ASV_16S_Phylprop <- merge_samples(physeq_filtered_16s, "group")
ASV_16S_Phylprop <- transform_sample_counts(ASV_16S_Phylprop, function(x) x/sum(x)*100)
ASV_16S_Phylprop <- tax_glom(ASV_16S_Phylprop, taxrank = "Phylum", NArm = FALSE)
plot_bar(ASV_16S_Phylprop, fill = "Phylum")
ggsave(paste(datafolder, "/Community/ASV_16S_BarPhylProp_city+topology.png"), device = png, width = 1920, height = 1080, units = "px", dpi = 144)
```

There seem to be some differences.

### Per city

```{r}
phy_rare_city <- merge_samples(phy_rare, "city")
plot_bar(phy_rare_city, fill = "Order") + 
  geom_bar(aes(color = Order, fill = Order), stat = "identity", position = "stack") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

```

### Split on Order

If you would want to make a subselection

```{r}
phy_rare_Mort <- subset_taxa(phy_rare, Order %in% c("Mortierellales"))
phy_rare_Mort@sam_data
plot_bar(phy_rare_Mort, x="Genus", fill = "Genus", facet_grid = "topology") +
  geom_bar(aes(color=Genus, fill=Genus), stat="identity", position="stack") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

```

Before moving on to statistics and stuff, let's do some visualization of data. First I would like to make a bar graph of diversity

## Presence Absence

Because I don't trust abundances, I'm going to make a presence absence OTU table.

```{r}
#phy_rare <- physeq_filtered_16s
phylo_pres_abs <- transform_sample_counts(physeq_filtered_16s, function(x) ifelse(x > 0, 1.00, 0))

phylo_pres_abs <- transform_sample_counts(phy_rare, function(x) ifelse(x > 0, 1.00, 0))

#Filter on ASVs that are present in at least two samples
#phylo_pres_abs <- prune_taxa(taxa_sums(phylo_pres_abs) > 2, phylo_pres_abs)
phylo_pres_abs
```

### Taxonomy Bar Graph function

```{r}
# Input should be presence absence physeq
library(phyloseq)
library(ggplot2)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(scales)
library(colorspace)

plot_grouped_taxa_bar <- function(ps_obj, 
                                  merge_var,
                                  level = "Class", 
                                  higher_level = "Phylum", 
                                  threshold = 3.5,
                                  palette = "Set2") {
  
   pretty_names <- function(x) {
    s <- gsub("_", " ", x)
    tools::toTitleCase(s)
  }
  
  # Merge and normalize
  ps_merged <- merge_samples(ps_obj, merge_var)
  ps_rel <- transform_sample_counts(ps_merged, function(x) x / sum(x) * 100)
  ps_tax <- tax_glom(ps_rel, taxrank = level, NArm = FALSE)
  
  # Clean taxonomy
  taxdf <- as.data.frame(tax_table(ps_tax))
  taxdf[[level]] <- as.character(taxdf[[level]])
  taxdf[[higher_level]] <- as.character(taxdf[[higher_level]])
  
  ambiguous <- is.na(taxdf[[higher_level]]) |
    tolower(taxdf[[higher_level]]) == "unknown" |
    grepl("incertae", taxdf[[higher_level]], ignore.case = TRUE) |
    grepl("unclassified", taxdf[[higher_level]], ignore.case = TRUE)
  taxdf[[higher_level]][ambiguous] <- "Unclassified"
  taxdf[[level]][ambiguous] <- "Unclassified"
  
  # Step 1: Collapse low-abundance phyla into a single group
  abund_phylum <- tapply(taxa_sums(ps_tax), taxdf[[higher_level]], sum)
  rel_abund_phylum <- abund_phylum / sum(abund_phylum) * 100
  low_phyla <- names(rel_abund_phylum[rel_abund_phylum < threshold])
  taxdf[[higher_level]][taxdf[[higher_level]] %in% low_phyla] <- "Other_Phyla"
  taxdf[[level]][taxdf[[higher_level]] == "Other_Phyla"] <- "Other_Phyla"
  
  # Step 2: Collapse low-abundance classes (only for remaining phyla)
  abund <- taxa_sums(ps_tax) / sum(taxa_sums(ps_tax)) * 100
  low_taxa <- setdiff(names(abund[abund < threshold]),
                      rownames(taxdf)[taxdf[[level]] %in% c("Unclassified", "Other_Phyla")])
  
  taxdf[[level]][rownames(taxdf) %in% low_taxa] <- paste0("Other_", taxdf[[higher_level]][rownames(taxdf) %in% low_taxa])
  # Collapse and reapply taxonomy
  tax_table(ps_tax) <- tax_table(as.matrix(taxdf))
  ps_tax <- tax_glom(ps_tax, taxrank = level)
  taxdf <- as.data.frame(tax_table(ps_tax))
  
  # Order phyla and classes by abundance
  abund_df <- psmelt(ps_tax)
  abund_summary <- aggregate(Abundance ~ get(higher_level) + get(level), data = abund_df, sum)
  colnames(abund_summary) <- c(higher_level, level, "Abundance")
  phyla_order <- aggregate(Abundance ~ get(higher_level), data = abund_summary, sum)
  colnames(phyla_order) <- c(higher_level, "Total")
  phyla_order <- phyla_order[order(-phyla_order$Total), ]
  
  taxdf[[higher_level]] <- factor(taxdf[[higher_level]], levels = phyla_order[[higher_level]])
  taxdf[[level]] <- factor(taxdf[[level]], levels = abund_summary[order(
    match(abund_summary[[higher_level]], phyla_order[[higher_level]]),
    -abund_summary$Abundance
  ), level])
  tax_table(ps_tax) <- tax_table(as.matrix(taxdf))
  
  # Color palette - use shuffled order just for color assignment
  phyla_ordered <- levels(taxdf[[higher_level]])
  phyla_shuffled <- phyla_ordered[order(nchar(phyla_ordered), phyla_ordered)]
  
  base_colors <- brewer.pal(min(length(phyla_shuffled), 8), palette)
  if (length(phyla_shuffled) > length(base_colors)) {
    base_colors <- colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(8, palette))(length(phyla_shuffled))
  }
  phylum_colors <- setNames(base_colors, phyla_shuffled)
  
  # Shades for class-level coloring (using original phylum order for logic)
  class_colors <- c()
  for (ph in phyla_ordered) {
    classes <- unique(taxdf[[level]][taxdf[[higher_level]] == ph])
    n <- length(classes)
    if (n > 0) {
      base_col <- phylum_colors[ph]
      shades <- colorRampPalette(c("gray90", base_col, "black"))(n + 4)[2:(n + 1)]
      class_colors <- c(class_colors, setNames(shades, classes))
    }
  }
  
  # Legend labels
  legend_labels <- setNames(
    pretty_names(paste0(names(class_colors), " (", taxdf[[higher_level]][match(names(class_colors), taxdf[[level]])], ")")),
    names(class_colors)
  )
  
  # Plot
  p <- plot_bar(ps_tax, fill = level, title = paste("Barplot of ",level," with abundance of more than ", threshold,"%"))
  p$data[[level]] <- factor(p$data[[level]], levels = levels(taxdf[[level]]))
  
  # Remove the original geom_bar and add a new one with no border
  p$layers <- NULL  # remove default geom_bar layer
  
  p <- p +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack", color = NA) +  # <- no borders
    scale_fill_manual(values = class_colors, labels = legend_labels) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
          legend.title = element_blank(),
          legend.text = element_text(size = 9)) +
    labs(y = "Relative Abundance (%)")
  return(p)
}

```

```{r}
plot_grouped_taxa_bar(ps_obj = phylo_pres_abs, merge_var = "topology", level = "Order", higher_level = "Phylum", palette = "Set3", threshold = 3.5)

```

#### Stats for taxonomy Dunn

```{r}

library(rstatix)
# Choose the taxonomic rank you're interested in
# e.g., "Genus", "Family", "Class", etc.
rank <- "Order"  # <-- change as needed
path <- "Output_path"
marker <- "16S"
# Extract OTU and taxonomy tables
asv_mat <- as(otu_table(phylo_pres_abs), "matrix")
if (taxa_are_rows(phylo_pres_abs)) {
  asv_mat <- t(asv_mat)
}
tax_mat <- as(tax_table(phylo_pres_abs), "matrix")
tax_mat[1:5,1:5]
## This is a matrix with all taxonomic info per ASV

# Make sure taxonomy column has no NAs or convert to "Unclassified"
tax_levels <- tax_mat[, rank]
tax_levels[is.na(tax_levels)] <- "Unclassified"
head(tax_levels)
# Now I make a list that sort of functions as a dictionairy in Python.

# Create a data frame with sample, ASV, presence, and taxonomy
asv_df <- as.data.frame(t(asv_mat))
asv_df$ASV <- rownames(t(asv_mat))  # Add ASV IDs as rownames
asv_df[1:5,1:5]

## Now, for each sample we want 
asv_long <- pivot_longer(asv_df, 
                         cols = -ASV, 
                         names_to = "Sample", 
                         values_to = "Presence") %>%
  filter(Presence >= 1)
#asv_long$Sample <- factor(asv_long$Sample, levels = unique(asv_long$Sample))

asv_long <- as.data.frame(asv_long)
#Something went wrong with the column names and I can't really seem to fix it easily. So a workaround:
#colnames(asv_long) <- c("Sample", "ASV", "Presence")
# Add taxonomy info
asv_long$Taxon <- tax_levels[asv_long$ASV]
asv_long[1:5,1:3]
# Count number of ASVs per taxon per sample
taxo_df <- as.data.frame(asv_long %>%
  group_by(Taxon, Sample) %>%
  summarise(ASV_count = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Taxon, values_from = ASV_count, values_fill = 0))
rownames(taxo_df) <- taxo_df$Sample
taxo_df$Sample <- NULL
# View result
head(taxo_df)

write.csv(taxo_df, paste(path,marker,rank,"_per_sample.png"), sep = ""))

### ANDS STATISTICS
## NON proportional
 tax_table_with_meta <- cbind(taxo_df, as.data.frame(phylo_pres_abs@sam_data))

## To make it proportional:
# enriched_meta <- read.csv(paste(datafolder,meta_11912_ITS_alpha.csv"), row.names = 1)
# tax_table_with_meta <- merge(taxo_df, enriched_meta, by = 'row.names')
# rownames(tax_table_with_meta) <- tax_table_with_meta$Row.names
# tax_table_with_meta$Row.names <- NULL
# # taxon_names <- names(taxo_df)
# tax_table_with_meta <- tax_table_with_meta %>%
#   mutate(across(names(taxo_df), ~ .x / Observed * 100))
# tax_table_with_meta[1:10,1:10]


# Ensure rows in func_table match sample order in meta_table
#category <- as.factor(phylo_pres_abs@sam_data$topology)


# Run Kruskal-Wallis test for each function
kw_results <- apply(tax_table_with_meta[1:ncol(taxo_df)], 2, function(x) kruskal.test(x ~ tax_table_with_meta$topology)$p.value)

# Adjust p-values for multiple testing (e.g., Benjamini-Hochberg)
kw_results_adj <- p.adjust(kw_results, method = "BH")

# View top significant functions
significant_taxa <- sort(kw_results_adj[kw_results_adj < 0.05])
names(significant_taxa)
str(names(significant_taxa))
taxo_df_sign <- tax_table_with_meta[,names(significant_taxa)]



pairwise_result_list <- list()

# Practise to do all pairwise comps.
# for (i in 1:length(names(func_table_signif))){
#   name <- names(signif_functions)[i]
#   pairwise_result <- pairwise_wilcox_test(
#     data.frame(value = func_table_signif[,i], group = category),
#     value ~ group,
#     p.adjust.method = "BH"
#     )
#   pairwise_result_list[name]<- pairwise_result
# }
#   pairwise_result

#One keeps giving an error, so I decided to just remove it.
#names(signif_functions[56])
#func_table_signif$aromatic_hydrocarbon_degradation <- NULL

taxo_df_sign_merge <-  merge(taxo_df_sign,phylo_pres_abs@sam_data,by="row.names",all.x=TRUE)
rownames(taxo_df_sign_merge) <- taxo_df_sign_merge$Row.names
taxo_df_sign_merge$Row.names <- NULL
# To make sure all names are correct (with spaces and stuff)
names(taxo_df_sign_merge) <- make.names(names(taxo_df_sign_merge))
names(taxo_df_sign) <- make.names(names(taxo_df_sign))

results_list <- list()

for (func in colnames(taxo_df_sign)) {
  print(func)
  
  # Perform Dunn's test (pairwise Kruskal-Wallis)
  try({
    test <- taxo_df_sign_merge %>%
      dunn_test(as.formula(paste(func, "~ topology")), p.adjust.method = "BH") %>%
      add_significance()
    
    # Add median direction info
    median_vals <- taxo_df_sign_merge %>%
      group_by(topology) %>%
      summarise(med = median(.data[[func]], na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")
    
    test <- test %>%
      left_join(median_vals, by = c("group1" = "topology")) %>%
      rename(med1 = med) %>%
      left_join(median_vals, by = c("group2" = "topology")) %>%
      rename(med2 = med) %>%
      mutate(direction = case_when(
        p.adj < 0.05 & med1 > med2 ~ paste(group1, ">", group2),
        p.adj < 0.05 & med1 < med2 ~ paste(group1, "<", group2),
        TRUE ~ "NS"
      )) %>%
      mutate(Function = func)
    
    results_list[[func]] <- test
  })
}


# Combine all test results
all_results <- bind_rows(results_list)
all_results$p.adj.global <- p.adjust(all_results$p, method = "BH")

write.csv(all_results, paste(path,"taxonomic_differences_statistics",marker, rank,".png"), sep =""))
```

### Typology

Now I'm wondering what we see if we make a sub selection based on typology

```{r}

#phylo_pres_abs <- transform_sample_counts(phy_rare, function(x) ifelse(x > 0, 1, 0))


#Merge the phyloseq by topoology
phy_rare_typology <- merge_samples(phylo_pres_abs, "topology")
sample_counts <- table(sample_data(phylo_pres_abs)$topology)
group_sizes <- sample_counts[sample_names(phy_rare_typology)]
phy_rare_typology@otu_table <- otu_table(sweep(phy_rare_typology@otu_table, 1, group_sizes, '/'), taxa_are_rows = taxa_are_rows(phy_rare_typology@otu_table))

p <- plot_heatmap(phy_rare_typology, method = "NMDS", distance = "bray", max.label = 400)

# Modify the fill scale with a custom midpoint at 0.5
p + scale_fill_gradient2(
  name = "Rel. Abundance",
  low = "black", mid = "yellow", high = "white",
  midpoint = 0.5, limits = c(0, 1)
)

topology_relative_Abundance <- write.csv(as.matrix(phy_rare_typology@otu_table),paste(datafolder,"Community_type/relative_abundance_per_topology"))
ggsave(paste(datafolder,"Community_type/ASV_ITS_heathmap_topology_relative.png"),width = 15, height =10,
units = "in", dpi = 600))
```

### city

```{r}
#Merge the phyloseq by topoology
phy_rare_typology <- merge_samples(phylo_pres_abs, "city")
sample_counts <- table(sample_data(phylo_pres_abs)$city)
group_sizes <- sample_counts[sample_names(phy_rare_typology)]
phy_rare_typology@otu_table <- otu_table(sweep(phy_rare_typology@otu_table, 1, group_sizes, '/'), taxa_are_rows = taxa_are_rows(phy_rare_typology@otu_table))


q <- plot_heatmap(phy_rare_typology, method = "NMDS", distance = "bray", max.label = 400)

# Modify the fill scale with a custom midpoint at 0.5
q + scale_fill_gradient2(
  name = "Rel. Abundance",
  low = "black", mid = "yellow", high = "white",
  midpoint = 0.5, limits = c(0, 1)
)

topology_relative_Abundance <- write.csv(as.matrix(phy_rare_typology@otu_table),paste(datafolder,"/Community_type/relative_abundance_per_city.csv"))
ggsave(paste(datafolder,"/Community_type/ASV_ITS_heathmap_city_relative.png"),width = 15, height =10,
units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

### Tree/No-Tree

```{r}
#Merge the phyloseq by topoology
phy_rare_typology <- merge_samples(phylo_pres_abs, "grond")
phy_rare_typology
sample_counts <- table(sample_data(phylo_pres_abs)$grond)
group_sizes <- sample_counts[sample_names(phy_rare_typology)]
phy_rare_typology@otu_table <- otu_table(sweep(phy_rare_typology@otu_table, 1, group_sizes, '/'), taxa_are_rows = taxa_are_rows(phy_rare_typology@otu_table))

check <- as.data.frame(phy_rare_typology@otu_table)

q <- plot_heatmap(phy_rare_typology, method = "NMDS", distance = "bray", max.label = 400)

# Modify the fill scale with a custom midpoint at 0.5
q + scale_fill_gradient2(
  name = "Rel. Abundance",
  low = "black", mid = "yellow", high = "white",
  midpoint = 0.5, limits = c(0, 1)
)

topology_relative_Abundance <- write.csv(as.matrix(phy_rare_typology@otu_table),paste(datafolder,"Community_type/relative_abundance_per_grond.csv"))
ggsave(paste(datafolder,"/Community_type/ASV_ITS_heathmap_grond_relative.png"),width = 15, height =10,
units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

## Heath maps

### Heath map all samples and ASVs

It's impossible to read this heath map, so we are going to make a subselection. We'll filter out all ASVs that are not abundant in any sample for more than 20% (change 0.20 to change percentage)

```{r}

physeq_abund <- filter_taxa(phy_rare, function(x) sum(x > 11912*0.05) > 0, TRUE)
physeq_abund
#This leaves us with 158 ASVs
```

Lets visualize the subselection of ASVs

```{r}
plot_heatmap(phylo_pres_abs, method = "NMDS", distance = "bray", max.label = 500)

ggsave(paste(datafolder,"/Community_type/ASV_ITS_heathmap_top005.png"),width = 15, height =10,
units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

### Merged per typology

```{r}
phy_rare_typology <- merge_samples(phylo_pres_abs, "topology")
physeq_abund <- filter_taxa(phy_rare_typology, function(x) sum(x > 11912*0.05) > 0, TRUE)
physeq_abund

plot_heatmap(phy_rare_typology, method = "NMDS", distance = "bray", max.label = 400)
ggsave(paste(datafolder,"/Community_type/ASV_ITS_heathmap_topology_pres_abs.png)",width = 15, height =10,
units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

### Per City

And also per city

```{r}
phy_rare_typology <- merge_samples(phy_rare, "city")
physeq_abund <- filter_taxa(phy_rare_typology, function(x) sum(x > 11912*0.05) > 0, TRUE)
physeq_abund

plot_heatmap(phy_rare_typology, method = "NMDS", distance = "bray", max.label = 400)

ggsave(paste(datafolder,"/Community_type/ASV_ITS_heathmap_city_all.png"),width = 15, height =10,
units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

### City and topology

Most interesting is ofcourse if the same typologies of cities cluster together. Merging data based on more than one classifier is, however, not standard in phyloseq.

```{r}
# Selection on two parameters} 
metadata <- sample_data(ps)
metadata$group <- paste(metadata$land_use, metadata$vegetation_type, sep = "_") sample_data(phy_rare) <- metadata 
phy_city_typ <- merge_samples(phy_rare, group = "group")
```

And than, again, plotting the heathmap

```{r}
physeq_abund <- filter_taxa(phy_city_typ, function(x) sum(x > 11912*0.10) > 0, TRUE)
physeq_abund

windows(10,20)
plot_heatmap(physeq_abund, method = "NMDS", distance = "bray", max.label = 500)
ggsave(paste(datafolder,"/Community_type/ASV_ITS_heathmap_city_topology_top10.png"),width = 15, height =10,
units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

## Indicator species analysis

```{r}
install.packages("indicspecies")  library(indicspecies) 
```

data processing

### indicator pres/abs

```{r}
#do some shuffling of the OTU table  
SpOTUFlip <- as.data.frame(t(phylo_pres_abs@otu_table)) 
#makes it a dataframe and puts x into y and y into x (flips it)    
#read in metadata  
metadata<-as.data.frame(phylo_pres_abs@sam_data) 
#read in metadata  
head(metadata) # check   
## Join based on SampleID  
SpOTU_Final<-merge(metadata, SpOTUFlip, by = 0)  
row.names(SpOTU_Final) <- SpOTU_Final$Row.names 
# join based on sample IDs, assuming they're the same for both OTU table and metadata   
SPotus_pres_ab = SpOTU_Final[,15:ncol(SpOTUFlip)]
#select just the ASV/OTU table part of the file (you may have to scroll to the back of the OTU file to find it...) 
SPtop = SpOTU_Final$topology #the metadata column group you care about  

set.seed(123)
SPind=multipatt(x=SPotus_pres_ab, cluster=SPtop, func = "indval.g", control = how(nperm=1000))   
summary(SPind, indvalcomp = T)
```

```{r}
library(indicspecies)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

extract_indicator_df <- function(multipatt_obj,
                                 p_thresh = 0.05,
                                 A_min = 0,
                                 B_min = 0,
                                 stat_min = 0,
                                 plot = TRUE,
                                 ref_A = NULL,
                                 ref_B = NULL) {
  
  # Extract main table
  df_sign <- as.data.frame(multipatt_obj$sign)
  df_sign$ASV <- rownames(df_sign)
  
  names(df_sign)[names(df_sign) == "stat"] <- "stat"
  names(df_sign)[names(df_sign) == "p.value"] <- "p.value"
  names(df_sign)[names(df_sign) == "index"] <- "combination"
  
  group_names <- colnames(multipatt_obj$A)
  
  all_rows <- list()
  
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(df_sign))) {
    asv <- df_sign$ASV[i]
    combo <- df_sign$combination[i]
   if (!is.na(combo) && combo > 0) {
  group <- group_names[combo]
  A_val <- multipatt_obj$A[asv, combo]
  B_val <- multipatt_obj$B[asv, combo]
  all_rows[[length(all_rows) + 1]] <- data.frame(
    ASV = asv,
    combination = group,
    A = A_val,
    B = B_val,
    stat = df_sign$stat[i],
    p.value = df_sign$p.value[i]
  )
}
  }
  
  df <- do.call(rbind, all_rows)
  
  df <- df %>%
    mutate(significance = cut(p.value,
                              breaks = c(-Inf, 0.001, 0.01, 0.05, Inf),
                              labels = c("Highly significant (p ≤ 0.001)",
                                         "Moderately significant (p ≤ 0.01)",
                                         "Marginally significant (p ≤ 0.05)",
                                         "Not significant"),
                              ordered_result = TRUE))
  
  df <- df %>%
    filter(p.value <= p_thresh,
           A >= A_min,
           B >= B_min,
           stat >= stat_min)
  
  if (plot) {
    p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = A, y = B, color = significance, label = ASV)) +
      geom_point(alpha = 0.8) +
      geom_text(vjust = -0.8, size = 3) +
      scale_size_continuous(range = c(3, 8)) +
      scale_color_manual(values = c(
        "Highly significant (p ≤ 0.001)" = "red",
        "Moderately significant (p ≤ 0.01)" = "orange",
        "Marginally significant (p ≤ 0.05)" = "gold",
        "Not significant" = "grey70"
      )) +
      labs(
        title = "Indicator Species Analysis",
        x = "Specificity (A)",
        y = "Fidelity (B)",
        color = "Significance",
      ) +
      mytheme +
      facet_wrap(~ combination)
    
    # Add reference lines if provided
    if (!is.null(ref_A)) {
      p <- p + geom_vline(xintercept = ref_A, linetype = "dashed", color = "blue")
    }
    if (!is.null(ref_B)) {
      p <- p + geom_hline(yintercept = ref_B, linetype = "dashed", color = "blue")
    }
    
    print(p)
  }
  
  return(df)
}

 
```

```{r}

Indicator_df_16S <- extract_indicator_df(multipatt_obj = SPind, plot = T)
#Now I would like taxonomic data to the ISA table
rownames(Indicator_df_16S) <- Indicator_df_16S$ASV
Indicator_df_16S$FeatureID <- Indicator_df_16S$ASV
Indicator_df_16S$ASV <- NULL

Taxonomic_table <- read.csv(paste(datafolder,"/taxonomy.csv"))
#Taxonomic_table$FeatureID <- rownames(Taxonomic_table)

Indicator_16S_with_taxonomy <- left_join(Indicator_df_16S, Taxonomic_table)
write.csv(Indicator_16S_with_taxonomy, paste(datafolder,"/indicator_species/indicator_species_16s_tax.csv"))

ggsave(paste(datafolder,"/indicator_species/indicator_species_16s.png"),width = 30, height =15, units = "in", dpi = 600)
write.csv(Indicator_df_16S, paste(datafolder,"/indicator_species/indicator_species_16s.csv"))

## Realized I have to do corrections, although it probably doesn't matter
Indicator_16S_with_taxonomy <- read.csv(paste(datafolder,"/indicator_species/indicator_species_16s_tax.csv"), row.names = 1)
# Adjust p-values using Benjamini-Hochberg (FDR)
Indicator_16S_with_taxonomy$p.adj <- p.adjust(
  Indicator_16S_with_taxonomy$p.value, 
  method = "fdr"
)
#write.csv(Indicator_16S_with_taxonomy, paste(datafolder,"/indicator_species/Indicator_species_16s_tax_BH.csv")
```

```{r}
Indicator_to_plot <- read.csv(paste(datafolder,"/indicator_species/ISA_A75_B25_16S_only_relevant.csv"))

library(ggrepel)
library(scales)
library(ggplot2)
library(viridis)
ggplot(Indicator_to_plot, aes(x = Specificity, y = Fidelity, 
                              color = ClassPhylum, label = ASV)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 6) +
  xlim(0.75,1.0) +
  geom_text_repel(size = 2, max.overlaps = 10, box.padding = 0.3) +  
  #scale_shape_manual(values=0:56) +
  #scale_color_manual(values = scales::hue_pal()(28)) +
  scale_color_viridis_d(option="turbo") +
  labs(
    title = "Indicator ASVs Analysis 16S",
    x = "Specificity (A)",
    y = "Fidelity (B)",
    color = "Class and Phylum"
  ) +
  mytheme +
  theme(legend.text = element_text(face = "italic")) +
 facet_wrap(~ combination)

ggsave(paste(datafolder,"/indicator_species/ISA_A75_B25_16S_only_relevant.png"),width = 30, height =15, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

### Visualization

```{r}
Indicator_to_plot <- read.csv(paste(datafolder,"/indicator_Species/ISA_ITS_A75_B25_named_onlyrelevant.csv"))


library(ggrepel)
library(scales)
ggplot(Indicator_to_plot, aes(x = Specificity, y = Fidelity, 
                              color = Name, shape = ClassPhylum, label = ASV)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 6) +
  xlim(0.75,1.0) +
  geom_text_repel(size = 2, max.overlaps = 70, box.padding = 0.3) +  
  scale_shape_manual(values=15:22) +
  #scale_color_manual(values = scales::hue_pal()(28)) +
  scale_color_viridis_d(option="turbo") +
  labs(
    title = "Indicator ASVs Analysis ITS",
    x = "Specificity (A)",
    y = "Fidelity (B)",
    color = "Species"
  ) +
  mytheme +
  theme(legend.text = element_text(face = "italic"))+
  facet_wrap(~ combination)
ggsave(paste(datafolder,"/indicator_Species/ISA_ITS_A75_B25_onlyrelevant.png"),width = 30, height =15, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

# Alpha_biodiversity

### Packages

```{r}
library(vegan)
library(data.table)
library(car)
```

Easy function to make a plot of alpha biodiversity

## Get richness values

Using this code, we can make a df with richness parameters. This we can use for statistics

```{r}
# phy_rare <- physeq_filtered_16s
# rm(physeq_filtered_16s)
Richnes_parameters <- estimate_richness(phy_rare, measures=c("Observed", "InvSimpson", "Shannon", "Chao1"))
phy_rare_typology <- merge_samples(phy_rare, "topology")
total_richness_typology <- estimate_richness(phy_rare_typology)
phy_rare_city <- merge_samples(phy_rare, "city")
total_richness_city <- estimate_richness(phy_rare_city)

# write.csv(total_richness_typology, paste(datafolder,"/ITS-11912/Alpha_diversity_graphs/ITS_total_diversity_typology.csv"))
write.csv(total_richness_city, paste(datafolder,"/16S_total_diversity_city.csv"))
```

## Add to metadata

It might be nice to add these richness values to the metadata, so it is in our phyloseq object

```{r}

# Assuming your phyloseq object is named 'physeq'
# Extract metadata from the phyloseq object
meta_data <- sample_data(phy_rare)

# Convert it to a data frame for manipulation if it's not already
meta_data_df <- as.data.frame(meta_data)

# Ensure rownames align between meta_data and Richness_parameters
# Merge the two data frames by their rownames
enriched_meta <- merge(meta_data_df, Richnes_parameters, by = "row.names", all.x = TRUE)

# Restore rownames after merging
rownames(enriched_meta) <- enriched_meta$Row.names
enriched_meta$Row.names <- NULL
enriched_meta <- as.matrix(enriched_meta)

# Convert the enriched data frame back to a sample_data object
sample_data(phy_rare) <- sample_data(enriched_meta)


# Your phyloseq object now contains the updated metadata
rm(meta_data_df)
write.csv(enriched_meta_df, paste(datafolder,"meta_11912_ITS_alpha.csv"))

rm(meta_data)


```

## Normaility and homogenity

N.B. SKIP THIS STEP AND GO TO FUNCTION STATISTICS, IF IT IS USABLE FOR YOUR DATA, IT'S WAY FASTER

To determine which statistical test we have to do, we first need to check if the data is normally distributed. We use the Shapiro-Wilkins test with H0 that the data is not normally distributed (so p \<0.05 = no normal distribution).

```{r}
shapiro.test(phy_rare@sam_data$Observed)
bartlett.test(Richnes_parameters$Observed, phy_rare@sam_data$group) 

```

To do it for certain groups use the following script:

```{r}
#metadata <- as.data.frame(sample_data(phy_rare))
topology_groups <- unique(enriched_meta$topology) # change to group you want to check, here it is topology, but might want to go for city/city-topology
normality_analysis <- list()

for (grp in topology_groups) {
  subset_data <- enriched_meta[enriched_meta$topology == grp, ]
  test_result <- shapiro.test(as.numeric(subset_data$Observed.y))
  if(test_result$p.value >0.05){
    bartlett_test_result <- bartlett.test(Observed.y ~ city, data = subset_data)
    normality_analysis[[grp]] <- append(test_result, bartlett_test_result)
    }else{
  normality_analysis[[grp]] <- test_result
  }
}

bartlett.test(Observed.y~topology, enriched_meta)
# Print results
normality_analysis
is.data.frame(metadata)
str(metadata)

## Only Treepit is normally distributed and Barlett test is also >0.05, so equeal variance
```

### Results Shapiro test

#### city

```         
$Amsterdam      Shapiro-Wilk normality test  data:  subset_data$Observed W = 0.87736, p-value = 3.722e-06   $Dordrecht      Shapiro-Wilk normality test  data:  subset_data$Observed W = 0.95869, p-value = 0.00722   $DenHaag      Shapiro-Wilk normality test  data:  subset_data$Observed W = 0.94888, p-value = 0.0002187   $Tilburg    Shapiro-Wilk normality test  data:  subset_data$Observed W = 0.91411, p-value = 1.327e-06   $Leiden     Shapiro-Wilk normality test  data:  subset_data$Observed W = 0.86617, p-value = 1.274e-05
```

#### Typology

```         
$Street_with_trees      Shapiro-Wilk normality test  data:  subset_data$Observed W = 0.96832, p-value = 0.02443   
$Sidewalk_with_trees    Shapiro-Wilk normality test  data:  subset_data$Observed W = 0.93774, p-value = 0.0002911   
$Street_without_trees   Shapiro-Wilk normality test  data:  subset_data$Observed W = 0.75396, p-value = 4.82e-11   
$Treepit    Shapiro-Wilk normality test  data:  subset_data$Observed W = 0.98188, p-value = 0.2307   
$Sidewalk_without_trees     Shapiro-Wilk normality test  data:  subset_data$Observed W = 0.74425, p-value = 5.244e-11
```

#### City-typology

```         
$Amsterdam_Street_with_trees    Shapiro-Wilk normality test  data:  subset_data$Observed W = 0.94177, p-value = 0.4051   
$Dordrecht_Street_with_trees    Shapiro-Wilk normality test  data:  subset_data$Observed W = 0.97701, p-value = 0.914   
$DenHaag_Sidewalk_with_trees    Shapiro-Wilk normality test  data:  subset_data$Observed W = 0.94303, p-value = 0.2087   
$Dordrecht_Sidewalk_with_trees      Shapiro-Wilk normality test  data:  subset_data$Observed W = 0.94014, p-value = 0.2914   
$DenHaag_Street_with_trees      Shapiro-Wilk normality test  data:  subset_data$Observed W = 0.95042, p-value = 0.2986   
$Amsterdam_Sidewalk_with_trees      Shapiro-Wilk normality test  data:  subset_data$Observed W = 0.93714, p-value = 0.3478   
$Tilburg_Sidewalk_with_trees    Shapiro-Wilk normality test  data:  subset_data$Observed W = 0.98343, p-value = 0.9497   
$Leiden_Sidewalk_with_trees     Shapiro-Wilk normality test  data:  subset_data$Observed W = 0.86858, p-value = 0.07432   
$Tilburg_Street_with_trees      Shapiro-Wilk normality test  data:  subset_data$Observed W = 0.95702, p-value = 0.3814   
$Leiden_Street_with_trees   Shapiro-Wilk normality test  data:  subset_data$Observed W = 0.91983, p-value = 0.2845   $Dordrecht_Street_without_trees    Shapiro-Wilk normality test  data:  subset_data$Observed W = 0.89709, p-value = 0.07221   
$Tilburg_Treepit    Shapiro-Wilk normality test  data:  subset_data$Observed W = 0.96608, p-value = 0.5961  
$Dordrecht_Sidewalk_without_trees   Shapiro-Wilk normality test  data:  subset_data$Observed W = 0.67278, p-value = 5.776e-05   $DenHaag_Sidewalk_without_trees     Shapiro-Wilk normality test  data:  subset_data$Observed W = 0.844, p-value = 0.002122   $Amsterdam_Sidewalk_without_trees      Shapiro-Wilk normality test  data:  subset_data$Observed W = 0.77159, p-value = 0.003196   $DenHaag_Street_without_trees    Shapiro-Wilk normality test  data:  subset_data$Observed W = 0.86791, p-value = 0.004788   $Tilburg_Sidewalk_without_trees      Shapiro-Wilk normality test  data:  subset_data$Observed W = 0.78324, p-value = 0.0002091   $Tilburg_Street_without_trees   Shapiro-Wilk normality test  data:  subset_data$Observed W = 0.87436, p-value = 0.006411   $Amsterdam_Street_without_trees      Shapiro-Wilk normality test  data:  subset_data$Observed W = 0.80445, p-value = 0.004196   
$DenHaag_Treepit    Shapiro-Wilk normality test  data:  subset_data$Observed W = 0.95827, p-value = 0.4047  
$Dordrecht_Treepit      Shapiro-Wilk normality test  data:  subset_data$Observed W = 0.96733, p-value = 0.7463   
$Leiden_Treepit     Shapiro-Wilk normality test  data:  subset_data$Observed W = 0.9411, p-value = 0.5125   
$Amsterdam_Treepit      Shapiro-Wilk normality test  data:  subset_data$Observed W = 0.90101, p-value = 0.09862   
$Leiden_Sidewalk_without_trees      Shapiro-Wilk normality test  data:  subset_data$Observed W = 0.90632, p-value = 0.2205   
$Leiden_Street_without_trees    Shapiro-Wilk normality test  data:  subset_data$Observed W = 0.90831, p-value = 0.203  
```

## Function statistics

Ofcourse, statistics have to be done on the results. If we have a normal distribution, we want to do a anova, otherwise we'll have to go for the Kruskall-Wallis

```{r}
# Input: 
#data = df, where variable1,variable2 and value need to be columnames. 
#variable1: What do you wanna look at, e.g. typology. 
#variable2: The thing you want to compare. so if var1 is typology, you can compare them by var2 (e.g. city)
#Value: Richness/shannon/etc.


compare_variables_function <- function(data, variable1, variable2, value) {
  statement_return <- list()
  
  # Loop through each level of the first grouping variable
  for (subgroup in unique(data[[variable1]])) {
    
    # Subset data for the current subgroup based on variable1
    subgroup_data <- data[data[[variable1]] == subgroup, ]
    
    # Check if subgroup_data is empty
    if (nrow(subgroup_data) == 0) {
      next
    }
    # Perform normality test (Shapiro-Wilk)
    normality_test <- shapiro.test(as.numeric(subgroup_data[[value]]))
    
    # Perform Bartlett test for homogeneity of variances
    bartlett_test <- bartlett.test(subgroup_data[[value]], subgroup_data[[variable2]])
    
    if (normality_test$p.value > 0.05 & bartlett_test$p.value > 0.05) {
      # If both normality and homogeneity are met, perform ANOVA
      anova_result <- aov(subgroup_data[[value]] ~ subgroup_data[[variable2]])
      tukey_result <- TukeyHSD(anova_result)
      statement_return[[subgroup]] <- list(anova_result = anova_result, tukey_result = tukey_result)
    } else {
      # If either normality or homogeneity is violated, perform non-parametric test
      kruskal_result <- kruskal.test(subgroup_data[[value]] ~ subgroup_data[[variable2]])
      wilcox_result <- pairwise.wilcox.test(subgroup_data[[value]], subgroup_data[[variable2]], p.adjust.method = "BH")
      #Added dunntest because that is the one that is preferably used
      dunntest_result <- dunnTest(subgroup_data[[value]] ~ subgroup_data[[variable2]], method = "bh")
      statement_return[[subgroup]] <- list(kruskal_result = kruskal_result, wilcox_result = wilcox_result, dunntest_result = dunntest_result)
    }
  }
  
  return(statement_return)
}
```

Oke, a little try with the dunntest instead of the wilcox

### Results function

Time to use the function

```{r}

stats.rich.typology.per.city<- compare_variables_function(data = enriched_meta, variable1 = "topology",variable2 = "city", value = "Observed")

stats.rich.city.per.typology <- compare_variables_function(data = enriched_meta, variable1 = "city",variable2 = "topology", value = "Observed")
stats.rich.city.per.typology
stats.rich.typology.per.city


```

```{r}
stats.rich.city.per.typology
stats.rich.typology.per.city
```

### shannon

```{r}

stats.rich.typology.per.city<- compare_variables_function(data = enriched_meta, variable1 = "topology",variable2 = "city", value = "Shannon")

stats.rich.city.per.typology <- compare_variables_function(data = enriched_meta, variable1 = "city",variable2 = "topology", value = "Shannon")
stats.rich.city.per.typology
stats.rich.typology.per.city


```

## Typology all

```{r}
kruskal.test(enriched_meta$Observed ~ enriched_meta$topology)
pairwise.wilcox.test(enriched_meta$Observed, enriched_meta$topology, p.adjust.method = "BH")


```

Calculate the mean per category

```{r}
meta_split <- split(enriched_meta, enriched_meta$topology)

for (i in meta_split){
  print(unique(i$topology))
  print(mean(i$Observed))
}
```

### Visualisation

If you start here with just the phyloseq object:

```{r}
enriched_meta <- sample_data(phy_rare)

```

![](http://127.0.0.1:17777/chunk_output/1366FAA9112f885a/39069BA7/cxyzmqr7pzlon/000014.png)

```{r}
richness_all_c <- ggplot(data = enriched_meta,
                         aes(x=topology, y = Observed, fill=topology))+
  geom_boxplot(lwd=0.1)+
  facet_grid(.~city, scales="free") +
  expand_limits(y=0:1000) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks=seq(0,1000, by=100)) +
  labs(x="City", y="Richness ASVs", title="ASV Richness ITS")+
  theme_bw()+
  scale_fill_manual(values=c('#FFABAB', '#ACE7FF', '#DBFFD6', '#ECD4FF', '#FEECC8')) +
  theme(text = element_text(size=12),legend.position = "right",
        legend.text = element_text(size=12),
        axis.title=element_text(size=14),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size=12, angle = 45, hjust = 0.5, vjust=0.5),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size=12),
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(), panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
richness_all_c

## If you want to add statistical groups quick and dirty
# richness_all_c <- richness_all_c + stat_compare_means(comparisons = list(c("Sidewalk_with_trees", "Sidewalk_without_trees"), c("Sidewalk_with_trees", "Street_with_trees"), c("Sidewalk_with_trees", "Treepit"), c("Sidewalk_without_trees", "Street_with_trees"),c("Sidewalk_without_trees", "Street_without_trees"),c("Sidewalk_without_trees", "Treepit"), c("Street_with_trees", "Street_without_trees"), c("Street_with_trees", "Treepit"), c("Street_without_trees","Treepit" )), 
#                             method = "wilcox.test", 
#                             label = "p.signif")
richness_all_c

ggsave(paste(datafolder,"/Alpha_diversity_graphs/ASV_richness_per_city.png"),width = 15, height =10,
units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

# Beta-Biodiversity

## Ordination/bray

```{r}
dstats <- function(x)(c(n=length(x), mean=mean(x), sd=sd(x), se=sd(x)/sqrt(length(x))))
```

Time to make NMDS. I choose this instead of PCoA because data is (generally) not normally distributed.

```{r}
 ITS_ordin_bray <- ordinate(phy_rare, "NMDS", "jaccard")
```

And also for 16s

```{r}



```

### NMDS all

```{r}
plot_ordination(phy_rare, ITS_ordin, type = "sample", color = "topology", shape= "city")
```

### NMDS - Subset

For only a subselection

```{r}
phy_Dordrecht <- subset_samples(phy_rare, city =="Dordrecht")
Dordrecht_ordin <- ordinate(phy_Dordrecht, "NMDS", "bray")
PlotDor<- plot_ordination(phy_Dordrecht, Dordrecht_ordin, type = "sample", color = "topology", shape= "streetname")
PlotDor + scale_shape_manual(values = c(0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8))
```

### NMDS merged

And one with merged samples, because it is easier to read.

```{r}
# IF necessary:
phy_city_typ <- merge_samples(phy_rare, group = "group")
# Update metadata for the aggregated samples
# We have to do some weird shit to make the metadata correct again, since there are lowercases in the topology name. Lesson for next time
merged_metadata <- row.names(sample_data(phy_city_typ))
sample_data(phy_city_typ)$city <- sub("_.*", "", merged_metadata)
sample_data(phy_city_typ)$topology <- sub("^[^_]+_", "", merged_metadata)
phy_city_typ@sam_data

city_type_ordin <- ordinate(phy_city_typ, method = "NMDS", distance = "bray")
plot_ordination(phy_city_typ, city_type_ordin, type = "sample", color = "city", shape= "topology")
```

### NMDS merged - error bars

Another try with merged samples, but than more statistically sound

```{r}
phy_rare@sam_data$group <- paste(phy_rare@sam_data$city, phy_rare@sam_data$topology, sep =" ")
group <- unique(phy_rare@sam_data$group)
```

```{r}
# First run the ordinal thing (ordinate(physeq_rare, method = "NMDS", distance = Bray))

ordination_coords <- as.data.frame(ITS_ordin$points)  # Use $points to extract NMDS coordinates
colnames(ordination_coords) <- paste0("NMDS", 1:ncol(ordination_coords))

#Add metadata
ordination_coords <- cbind(ordination_coords, sample_data(phy_rare))


#Summarize
# Example: Summarize by "Category" in the metadata
summary_coords <- ordination_coords %>%
  group_by(group) %>% # Replace "Category" with your actual grouping variable
  summarise(
    PC1_mean = mean(NMDS1),
    PC2_mean = mean(NMDS2),
    PC1_sd = sd(NMDS1),
    PC2_sd = sd(NMDS2)
  )
## We now have a df with all mean coordinates. To make the legend/colours work we only need to add the metadata to it
summary_coords$city <- sub("_.*", "", summary_coords$group)
summary_coords$topology <- sub("^[^_]+_", "", summary_coords$group)

# And plot the NMDS
ggplot(summary_coords, aes(x = PC1_mean, y = PC2_mean, color = phy_rare@sam_data$topology, shape = phy_rare@sam_data$city)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) + # Mean points
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = PC2_mean - PC2_sd, ymax = PC2_mean + PC2_sd), width = 0.1) + # Vertical error bars
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = PC1_mean - PC1_sd, xmax = PC1_mean + PC1_sd), height = 0.1) + # Horizontal error bars
  labs(x = "PCoA", y = "PC2", title = "PCoA with Grouped Error Bars") +
  theme_minimal()

ggsave(paste(datafolder,"NMDS_error_bars.png"),width = 15, height = 5,
units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

Dordrecht is an outlier here, which is something I've seen before. This seems to be caused by the streets that do have trees in them, but further away.

Another try at making this figure

```{r}
mytheme <- theme_bw()+
  theme(text = element_text(size=15),
        strip.background = element_blank(),
        strip.placement = "outside",
        legend.box.background = element_rect(),
        legend.box.margin = margin(1, 1, 1, 1),
        legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(), 
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

av_pcoa_bray_all_city <- ggplot(summary_coords, aes(x=x.mean, y=y.mean, fill=topology, shape=city)) +
  facet_grid(.~city, scales="free_x") +
  geom_point(size=5,alpha=0.7) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=y.mean-y.se, ymax=y.mean+y.se)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(xmin=x.mean-x.se, xmax=x.mean+x.se)) +
  labs(x=paste("PCoA: ", round(re_comall$values$Relative_eig[1]*100, 2), "%", sep=""),
       y=paste("PCoA: ", round(re_comall$values$Relative_eig[2]*100, 2), "%", sep=""),
       title= "Bray Curtis") +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c('#FFABAB', '#ACE7FF', '#DBFFD6', '#ECD4FF', '#FEECC8')) +
  scale_shape_manual(values=c(21,21,21,21,21)) +
  guides(fill=guide_legend(override.aes=list(shape=21)))+
  mytheme
av_pcoa_bray_all_city

```

## 16S-PCoA

First make sure we have a metadata df

```{r}
enriched_meta <- data.frame(sample_data(phy_rare))

write.csv(enriched_meta, paste(datafolder,"/metadate_16s.csv"))
```

```{r}
library(doBy)
#PCOA Bray all-------------
# phy_rare <- physeq_filtered_16s
# rm(physeq_filtered_16s)
S16_ordin_jac <- ordinate(phy_rare, method = "PCoA", distance = "bray")

#enriched_meta <- as.matrix(sample_data(phy_rare))

df_bray_comall_jac <- merge(S16_ordin_jac$vectors[,1:10], enriched_meta, by = "row.names")
row.names(df_bray_comall_jac) <- df_bray_comall_jac$Row.names
df_bray_comall_jac <- df_bray_comall_jac[, -1] 


str(df_bray_comall_jac)


mytheme <- theme_bw()+
  theme(text = element_text(size=15),
        strip.background = element_blank(),
        strip.placement = "outside",
        legend.box.background = element_rect(),
        legend.box.margin = margin(1, 1, 1, 1),
        legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(), 
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank()) 

pcoa_bray_all <- ggplot(df_bray_comall_jac, aes(Axis.1,Axis.5, fill=topology, shape=city)) +
  geom_point(size=5, alpha=0.7)+
  labs(x=paste("PCoA1: ", round(S16_ordin_jac$values$Relative_eig[1] * 100, 2), "%", sep=""),
       y=paste("PCoA2: ", round(S16_ordin_jac$values$Relative_eig[5] * 100, 2), "%", sep=""),
       title="Jaccard") +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c('#FFABAB', '#ACE7FF', '#DBFFD6', '#ECD4FF', '#FEECC8')) +
  scale_shape_manual(values=c(21,22,23,24,25)) +
  guides(fill=guide_legend(override.aes=list(shape=21)))+
  mytheme

pcoa_bray_all
ggsave(paste(datafolder,"pcoa/PCOA_TESTINGAXIS.png"),width = 15, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

#No I want to add error-bars
dstats <- function(x)(c(n=length(x), mean=mean(x), sd=sd(x), se=sd(x)/sqrt(length(x))))
# X_summary <- summaryBy(Axis.1~city+topology, data=df_bray_comall_jac, FUN=dstats)
# Y_summary <- summaryBy(Axis.2~city+topology, data=df_bray_comall_jac, FUN=dstats)
# df_bray_comall_jac_mean <- cbind(X_summary,Y_summary[,3:6])
X_summary <- summaryBy(Axis.1~city+topology, data=df_bray_comall_jac, FUN=dstats)
Y_summary <- summaryBy(Axis.5~city+topology, data=df_bray_comall_jac, FUN=dstats)
df_bray_comall_jac_mean <- cbind(X_summary,Y_summary[,3:6])

## And one with Error bars
pcoa_bray_all_mean <- ggplot(df_bray_comall_jac_mean, aes(Axis.5.mean,Axis.1.mean, fill=topology, shape=city)) +
  #facet_grid(.~city, scales="free_x") +
  
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=Axis.1.mean-Axis.1.se, ymax=Axis.1.mean+Axis.1.se), linewidth =0.7) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(xmin=Axis.5.mean-Axis.5.se, xmax=Axis.5.mean+Axis.5.se), linewidth = 0.7) +
  labs(x=paste("PCoA1: ", round(S16_ordin_jac$values$Relative_eig[1] * 100, 2), "%", sep=""),
       y=paste("PCoA2: ", round(S16_ordin_jac$values$Relative_eig[5] * 100, 2), "%", sep=""),
       title="Bray") +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c('#FFABAB', '#ACE7FF', '#DBFFD6', '#ECD4FF', '#FEECC8')) +
  geom_point(size=5, alpha=0.7)+
  scale_shape_manual(values=c(21,22,23,24,25)) +
  guides(fill=guide_legend(override.aes=list(shape=21)))+
  mytheme

pcoa_bray_all_mean
ggsave(paste(datafolder,"pcoa/PCOA_per_city_error_bars_BRAY_extraa.png"),width = 15, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

```

In ITSthere are some outliers, so I want to export the df with coordinates to check which one they are

```{r}
write.csv(df_bray_comall_jac, paste(datafolder,"pcoa/S16_PCoA_coordinates_BRAY.csv"))
```

All outliers are in three streets in The Hague. I've decided to remove them from my data for a little test .

### Statistics beta - Permanova

To make check statistical relationships, it's time to do a test

```{r}
# Calculate jaccard distance matrix
#jaccard_dist <- phyloseq::distance(phy_rare, method = "bray")
library(RVAideMemoire)
enriched_meta$covertree <- paste(enriched_meta$covering, enriched_meta$type_street)
# PERMANOVA for individual variables
adonis_city <- adonis2(jaccard_dist ~ city, data=enriched_meta)
adonis_city
adonis_topology <- adonis2(jaccard_dist ~ topology, data= enriched_meta)
adonis_topology
adonis_streetname <- adonis2(jaccard_dist ~ streetname, data= enriched_meta)
adonis_streetname
adonis_covering <- adonis2(jaccard_dist ~ covering, data= enriched_meta)
adonis_covering
adonis_typestraat <- adonis2(jaccard_dist ~ type_street, data= enriched_meta)
adonis_covering
adonis_covertree <- adonis2(jaccard_dist ~ covertree, data = enriched_meta)
adonis_covertree
###Permanova assumes equal dispersion amongst groups
#beta_disp <- betadisper(jaccard_dist, meta$Group)
#anova(beta_disp)  # Tests if dispersion is significantly different
#permutest(beta_disp)  # Permutation test for dispersion

# PERMANOVA for interaction (combination)
# adonis_combination <- adonis2(jaccard_dist ~ group, data =enriched_meta)

PairPermS16 <- pairwise.perm.manova(jaccard_dist, enriched_meta$city, test="Pillai", nperm = 999, p.method = "fdr", F = TRUE, R2 = TRUE)
PairPermS16
PairPermS16$F.value

pairpermS162 <- pairwise.perm.manova(jaccard_dist, enriched_meta$topology, test="Pillai", nperm = 999, p.method = "fdr", F = TRUE, R2 = TRUE)
pairpermS162
pairpermS162$F.value

pairperm_covertree <- pairwise.perm.manova(jaccard_dist, enriched_meta$covertree, test = "pillai", nperm =999, p.method ="fdr", F=T, R2=T)
pairperm_covertree$R2.value
## For pairwase manova:
# higher F-value means that between-group variation is alrger relative to the within group variation
# This generally means a stronger effect of the grouping variable
# Display results

# Flatten and bind rows
combined_results <- bind_rows(
  data.frame(Test = "Adonis_topology", adonis_topology),
  data.frame(Test="Pairwise_permanova_topology", pairpermS162$p.value),
  data.frame(Test = "Pairwise_topology_R2", pairpermS162$R2.value),
  data.frame(Test = "Adonis_city", adonis_city),
  data.frame(Test = "Pairwise_permanova_city", PairPermS16$p.value),
  data.frame(Test = "R2_value_city", PairPermS16$R2.value),
  data.frame(Test = "Adonis_streetname", adonis_streetname),
  data.frame(Test = "Adonis_covering", adonis_covering),
  data.frame(Test = "Adonis_typestreet", adonis_typestraat),
  data.frame(Test = "Adonis_covertree", adonis_covertree),
  data.frame(Test = "Pairwise_covertree", pairperm_covertree$p.value),
  data.frame(Test = "R2_covertree", pairperm_covertree$R2.value),
  .id = "Source"
)




write.csv(combined_results, paste(datafolder,"/pcoa/statistical_summary_BRAY.csv"))




```

```         
Permutation test for adonis under reduced model Permutation: free Number of permutations:  999  adonis2(formula = bray_dist ~ enriched_meta$city)           Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)     Model      4    5.448 0.02573 2.9576  0.001 *** Residual 448  206.315 0.97427                   Total    452  211.763 1.00000                   --- Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1 Permutation test for adonis under reduced model Permutation: free Number of permutations: 999  adonis2(formula = bray_dist ~ enriched_meta$topology)           Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)     Model      4    9.391 0.04435 5.1971  0.001 *** Residual 448  202.373 0.95565                   Total    452  211.763 1.00000                   --- Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1 Permutation test for adonis under reduced model Permutation: free Number of permutations: 999  adonis2(formula = bray_dist ~ enriched_meta$group)           Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)     Model     24   24.613 0.11623 2.3454  0.001 *** Residual 428  187.150 0.88377                   Total    452  211.763 1.00000                   --- Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1
```

And the same statistics, but than without outliers

```{r}
# Calculate Bray-Curtis distance matrix
jaccard_dist <- phyloseq::distance(physeq_filtered, method = "jaccard")

# PERMANOVA for individual variables
adonis_city <- adonis2(jaccard_dist ~ city, data=filtered_enriched_meta)
adonis_topology <- adonis2(jaccard_dist ~ topology, data= filtered_enriched_meta)

###Permanova assumes equal dispersion amongst groups
#beta_disp <- betadisper(jaccard_dist, meta$Group)
#anova(beta_disp)  # Tests if dispersion is significantly different
#permutest(beta_disp)  # Permutation test for dispersion

# PERMANOVA for interaction (combination)
adonis_combination <- adonis2(jaccard_dist ~ group, data =filtered_enriched_meta)

PairPermS16 <- pairwise.perm.manova(jaccard_dist, filtered_enriched_meta$city, test="Pillai", nperm = 999, p.method = "fdr", F = TRUE, R2 = TRUE)
PairPermS16
PairPermS16$F.value

pairpermS162 <- pairwise.perm.manova(jaccard_dist, filtered_enriched_meta$topology, test="Pillai", nperm = 999, p.method = "fdr", F = TRUE, R2 = TRUE)
pairpermS162
pairpermS162$F.value
## For pairwase manova:
# higher F-value means that between-group variation is alrger relative to the within group variation
# This generally means a stronger effect of the grouping variable
# Display results
print(adonis_city)
print(adonis_topology)
print(adonis_combination)
```

## ITS PCoA

```{r}
library(doBy)
library(ggplot2)
library(vegan)
library(phyloseq)
enriched_meta <- read.csv(paste(datafolder,"ITS-11912/meta_11912_ITS_alpha.png"), row.names = 1)
enriched_meta$covertree <- paste(enriched_meta$type_straat, enriched_meta$bedekking)

#PCOA Bray all-------------
ITS_ordin_jac <- ordinate(phy_rare, method = "PCoA", distance = "bray")


df_bray_comall_jac <- merge(ITS_ordin_jac$vectors[,1:2], enriched_meta, by = "row.names")
row.names(df_bray_comall_jac) <- df_bray_comall_jac$Row.names
df_bray_comall_jac <- df_bray_comall_jac[, -1] 


str(df_bray_comall_jac)


mytheme <- theme_bw()+
  theme(text = element_text(size=15, face ="bold"),
        strip.background = element_blank(),
        strip.placement = "outside",
        legend.box.background = element_rect(),
        legend.box.margin = margin(1, 1, 1, 1),
        legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(), 
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(linewidth = 2)) 

pcoa_bray_all <- ggplot(df_bray_comall_jac, aes(Axis.1,Axis.2, fill=topology, shape=city)) +
  geom_point(size=5, alpha=0.7)+
  labs(x=paste("PCoA1: ", round(ITS_ordin_jac$values$Relative_eig[1] * 100, 2), "%", sep=""),
       y=paste("PCoA2: ", round(ITS_ordin_jac$values$Relative_eig[2] * 100, 2), "%", sep=""),
       title="Bray") +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c('#FFABAB', '#ACE7FF', '#DBFFD6', '#ECD4FF', '#FEECC8')) +
  scale_shape_manual(values=c(21,22,23,24,25)) +
  guides(fill=guide_legend(override.aes=list(shape=21)))+
  mytheme

pcoa_bray_all
#ggsave(paste(datafolder,"ITS-11912/pcoa/PCOA_NO_ERROR_BRAY.png"),width = 15, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

#No I want to add error-bars
dstats <- function(x)(c(n=length(x), mean=mean(x), sd=sd(x), se=sd(x)/sqrt(length(x))))
X_summary <- summaryBy(Axis.1~city+topology, data=df_bray_comall_jac, FUN=dstats)
Y_summary <- summaryBy(Axis.2~city+topology, data=df_bray_comall_jac, FUN=dstats)
df_bray_comall_jac_mean <- cbind(X_summary,Y_summary[,3:6])

## And one with Error bars
pcoa_bray_all_mean <- ggplot(df_bray_comall_jac_mean, aes(Axis.1.mean,Axis.2.mean, fill=topology, shape=city)) +
  #facet_grid(.~city, scales="free_x") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=Axis.2.mean-Axis.2.se, ymax=Axis.2.mean+Axis.2.se), linewidth=.7) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(xmin=Axis.1.mean-Axis.1.se, xmax=Axis.1.mean+Axis.1.se), linewidth=.7) +
  geom_point(size=5, alpha=0.7, stroke = 1)+
  labs(x=paste("PCoA1: ", round(ITS_ordin_jac$values$Relative_eig[1] * 100, 2), "%", sep=""),
       y=paste("PCoA2: ", round(ITS_ordin_jac$values$Relative_eig[2] * 100, 2), "%", sep=""),
       title="Bray") +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c('#FFABAB', '#ACE7FF', '#DBFFD6', '#ECD4FF', '#FEECC8')) +
  scale_shape_manual(values=c(21,22,23,24,25)) +
  guides(fill=guide_legend(override.aes=list(shape=21)))+
  mytheme

pcoa_bray_all_mean
ggsave(paste(datafolder,"ITS-11912/pcoa/PCOA_error_bars_BRAY_NoCross.png"),width = 15, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

```

In ITS there are some outliers, so I want to export the df with coordinates to check which one they are

```{r}
write.csv(df_bray_comall_jac, paste(datafolder,"ITS-11912/pcoa/ITS_PCoA_coordinates_BRAY.csv")
```

All outliers are in three streets in The Hague. I've decided to remove them from my data for a little test .

```{r}
#filter phyloseq
physeq_filtered <- subset_samples(phy_rare, !grepl("^H9|^H7|^H6BS1|^H6BS2", sample_names(phy_rare)))
#filter metadata
filtered_enriched_meta  <- enriched_meta[!grepl("^H9|^H7|^H6BS1|^H6BS2", rownames(enriched_meta)), ]
## Repeat ordination
filtered_ITS_ordin_jac <- ordinate(physeq_filtered, method = "PCoA", distance = "bray")

#Clean df
filtered_bray_comall_jac <- merge(filtered_ITS_ordin_jac$vectors[,1:2], filtered_enriched_meta, by = "row.names")
row.names(filtered_bray_comall_jac) <- filtered_bray_comall_jac$Row.names
filtered_bray_comall_jac <- filtered_bray_comall_jac[, -1] 

## Now make a new graph
pcoa_bray_all_filt <- ggplot(filtered_bray_comall_jac, aes(Axis.1,Axis.2, fill=topology, shape=city)) +
  geom_point(size=5, alpha=0.7)+
  labs(x=paste("PCoA1: ", round(filtered_ITS_ordin_jac$values$Relative_eig[1] * 100, 2), "%", sep=""),
       y=paste("PCoA2: ", round(filtered_ITS_ordin_jac$values$Relative_eig[2] * 100, 2), "%", sep=""),
       title="Bray") +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c('#FFABAB', '#ACE7FF', '#DBFFD6', '#ECD4FF', '#FEECC8')) +
  scale_shape_manual(values=c(21,22,23,24,25)) +
  guides(fill=guide_legend(override.aes=list(shape=21)))+
  mytheme

pcoa_bray_all_filt
 ggsave(paste(datafolder,"ITS-11912/pcoa/PCOA_NO_ERROR_outliers_rem_BRAY.png"),width = 15, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)
 
 
 #Now I want to add error-bars
dstats <- function(x)(c(n=length(x), mean=mean(x), sd=sd(x), se=sd(x)/sqrt(length(x))))
X_summary <- summaryBy(Axis.1~city+topology, data=filtered_bray_comall_jac, FUN=dstats)
Y_summary <- summaryBy(Axis.2~city+topology, data=filtered_bray_comall_jac, FUN=dstats)
df_bray_comall_jac_mean <- cbind(X_summary,Y_summary[,3:6])
 ## And one with Error bars
pcoa_bray_all_mean <- ggplot(df_bray_comall_jac_mean, aes(Axis.1.mean,Axis.2.mean, fill=topology, shape=city)) +
  #facet_grid(.~city, scales="free_x") +
  geom_point(size=5, alpha=0.7)+
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=Axis.2.mean-Axis.2.se, ymax=Axis.2.mean+Axis.2.se)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(xmin=Axis.1.mean-Axis.1.se, xmax=Axis.1.mean+Axis.1.se)) +
  labs(x=paste("PCoA1: ", round(ITS_ordin_jac$values$Relative_eig[1] * 100, 2), "%", sep=""),
       y=paste("PCoA2: ", round(ITS_ordin_jac$values$Relative_eig[2] * 100, 2), "%", sep=""),
       title="Bray") +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c('#FFABAB', '#ACE7FF', '#DBFFD6', '#ECD4FF', '#FEECC8')) +
  scale_shape_manual(values=c(21,22,23,24,25)) +
  guides(fill=guide_legend(override.aes=list(shape=21)))+
  mytheme

pcoa_bray_all_mean
ggsave(paste(datafolder,"ITS-11912/pcoa/PCOA_nooutliers_error_bars_Bray.png"),width = 25, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

### Statistics beta - Permanova

To make check statistical relationships, it's time to do a test

```{r}
library(RVAideMemoire)
# Calculate Bray-Curtis distance matrix
#jaccard_dist <- phyloseq::distance(phy_rare, method = "bray")
#enriched_meta <- as.data.frame(enriched_meta)
# PERMANOVA for individual variables
adonis_city <- adonis2(jaccard_dist ~ city, data=enriched_meta)
adonis_city
adonis_topology <- adonis2(jaccard_dist ~ topology, data= enriched_meta)
adonis_topology
adonis_streetname <- adonis2(jaccard_dist ~ streetname, data= enriched_meta)
adonis_streetname
adonis_covering <- adonis2(jaccard_dist ~ bedekking, data= enriched_meta)
adonis_covering
adonis_typestraat <- adonis2(jaccard_dist ~ type_straat, data= enriched_meta)
adonis_covering
adonis_covertree <- adonis2(jaccard_dist ~ covertree, data = enriched_meta)
adonis_covertree
###Permanova assumes equal dispersion amongst groups
#beta_disp <- betadisper(jaccard_dist, meta$Group)
#anova(beta_disp)  # Tests if dispersion is significantly different
#permutest(beta_disp)  # Permutation test for dispersion

# PERMANOVA for interaction (combination)
adonis_combination <- adonis2(jaccard_dist ~ group, data =enriched_meta)

PairPermITS <- pairwise.perm.manova(jaccard_dist, enriched_meta$city, test="Pillai", nperm = 999, p.method = "fdr", F = TRUE, R2 = TRUE)
PairPermITS
PairPermITS$F.value

pairpermits2 <- pairwise.perm.manova(jaccard_dist, enriched_meta$topology, test="Pillai", nperm = 999, p.method = "fdr", F = TRUE, R2 = TRUE)
pairpermits2
pairpermits2$F.value

pairperm_covertree <- pairwise.perm.manova(jaccard_dist, enriched_meta$covertree, test = "pillai", nperm =999, p.method ="fdr", F=T, R2=T)
pairperm_covertree$R2.value

## For pairwase manova:
# higher F-value means that between-group variation is alrger relative to the within group variation
# This generally means a stronger effect of the grouping variable
# Display results

# Flatten and bind rows
combined_results <- bind_rows(
  data.frame(Test = "Adonis_topology", adonis_topology),
  data.frame(Test="Pairwise_permanova_topology", pairpermits2$p.value),
  data.frame(Test = "Pairwise_topology_R2", pairpermits2$R2.value),
  data.frame(Test = "Adonis_city", adonis_city),
  data.frame(Test = "Pairwise_permanova_city", PairPermITS$p.value),
  data.frame(Test = "R2_value_city", PairPermITS$R2.value),
  data.frame(Test = "Adonis_streetname", adonis_streetname),
  data.frame(Test = "Adonis_covering", adonis_covering),
  data.frame(Test = "Adonis_typestreet", adonis_typestraat),
  data.frame(Test = "Adonis_covertree", adonis_covertree),
  data.frame(Test = "Pairwise_covertree", pairperm_covertree$p.value),
  data.frame(Test = "R2_covertree", pairperm_covertree$R2.value),
  .id = "Source"
)


write.csv(combined_results, paste(datafolder,"ITS-11912/pcoa/statistical_summary_BRAY.csv")




```

```         
Permutation test for adonis under reduced model Permutation: free Number of permutations:  999  adonis2(formula = bray_dist ~ enriched_meta$city)           Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)     Model      4    5.448 0.02573 2.9576  0.001 *** Residual 448  206.315 0.97427                   Total    452  211.763 1.00000                   --- Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1 Permutation test for adonis under reduced model Permutation: free Number of permutations: 999  adonis2(formula = bray_dist ~ enriched_meta$topology)           Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)     Model      4    9.391 0.04435 5.1971  0.001 *** Residual 448  202.373 0.95565                   Total    452  211.763 1.00000                   --- Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1 Permutation test for adonis under reduced model Permutation: free Number of permutations: 999  adonis2(formula = bray_dist ~ enriched_meta$group)           Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)     Model     24   24.613 0.11623 2.3454  0.001 *** Residual 428  187.150 0.88377                   Total    452  211.763 1.00000                   --- Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1
```

And the same statistics, but than without outliers

```{r}
# Calculate Bray-Curtis distance matrix
bray_dist <- phyloseq::distance(physeq_filtered, method = "bray")

# PERMANOVA for individual variables
adonis_city <- adonis2(bray_dist ~ city, data=filtered_enriched_meta)
adonis_topology <- adonis2(bray_dist ~ topology, data= filtered_enriched_meta)

###Permanova assumes equal dispersion amongst groups
#beta_disp <- betadisper(jaccard_dist, meta$Group)
#anova(beta_disp)  # Tests if dispersion is significantly different
#permutest(beta_disp)  # Permutation test for dispersion

# PERMANOVA for interaction (combination)
adonis_combination <- adonis2(bray_dist ~ group, data =filtered_enriched_meta)

PairPermITS <- pairwise.perm.manova(bray_dist, filtered_enriched_meta$city, test="Pillai", nperm = 999, p.method = "fdr", F = TRUE, R2 = TRUE)
PairPermITS
PairPermITS$F.value

pairpermits2 <- pairwise.perm.manova(bray_dist, filtered_enriched_meta$topology, test="Pillai", nperm = 999, p.method = "fdr", F = TRUE, R2 = TRUE)
pairpermits2
pairpermits2$F.value
## For pairwase manova:
# higher F-value means that between-group variation is alrger relative to the within group variation
# This generally means a stronger effect of the grouping variable
# Display results
print(adonis_city)
print(adonis_topology)
print(adonis_combination)

combined_results <- bind_rows(
  data.frame(Test = "Adonis_topology", adonis_topology),
  data.frame(Test = "Pairwise_permanova_city", PairPermITS$p.value),
  data.frame(Test = "F_value_city", PairPermITS$F.value),
  data.frame(Test="Pairwise_permanova_topology", pairpermits2$p.value),
  data.frame(Test = "Adonis_typestreet", adonis_typestraat),
  .id = "Source"
)


write.csv(combined_results, paste(datafolder,"ITS-11912/pcoa/statistical_summary_NO_OUTLIERS_BRAY.csv")

```

## RC-BRAY

```{r}
library(iCAMP)
phy_rare <- physeq_filtered_16s

set.seed(123)
ASV_com_bact <- t(as.data.frame(phy_rare@otu_table))
metacommunity_df <- data.frame(metacommunity = phy_rare@sam_data$topology)
rownames(metacommunity_df) <- rownames(phy_rare@sam_data)
rc_bray_bact <- RC.cm(com = ASV_com_bact, rand=999, meta.group = metacommunity_df)

hist(rc_bray_bact$index, breaks = 50, main = "RC Bray Distribution", xlab = "RC Bray")
table(rc_bray_bact$sig.index) / length(rc_bray_bact$sig.index)
save.image(paste(datafolder,"/R-scripts/Bacteria_RCBRAY_cd.Rdata"))

```

## Functional analysis

### 16s

```{r}
library(microeco)
library(file2meco)
library(vegan)
library(microbiome)

# Loading the FAPROTAX database is more complex. To calculate annotation, we first transform the phyloseq to the meco object
Meco_16S_Rare   <- phyloseq2meco(physeq_filtered_16s)
Meco_16S_Func   <- trans_func$new(dataset =Meco_16S_Rare)


# Calculate the FAPROTAX 1/0 matrix and extraction 
Meco_16S_Func$cal_spe_func(prok_database = "FAPROTAX")
Func16S         <- as.data.frame(Meco_16S_Func$res_spe_func)
sum(rowSums(Func16S, na.rm = F) == 0)
sum(rowSums(Func16S, na.rm = F) != 0)
sum(rowSums(Func16S, na.rm = F) != 0)/length(rownames(Func16S))
## 26688 ASV are annotated which is 17%

# Note that I will continue with this microeco package, as translating this back into phyloseq is not ideal
```

#### Bar Graph/Heat Maps

```{r}
# Calculating percentage coverage and making a heatmap
Meco_16S_Func$cal_spe_func_perc(abundance_weighted = TRUE, perc = TRUE, dec = 8)
Func16SPerc     <- as.data.frame(Meco_16S_Func$res_spe_func_perc)

# creating heatmap x axis order from Ordination (same as phyloseq heatmap)
## extracting ordination coordinates and calculating radial theta
Ord16STest      <- Meco_16S_Rare$clone()
Ord16STest$cal_betadiv(method = "jaccard")
Ord16SBeta_group      <- trans_beta$new(dataset = Ord16STest, group = "group", measure = "jaccard")
Ord16SBeta$cal_ordination(method = "PCoA")
Scores          <- scores(Ord16SBeta$res_ordination, choices = c(1,2), display = "sites")
Order16S        <- Ord16STest$sample_names()[order(radial_theta(Scores))]

rm(Ord16STest, Ord16SBeta, Scores)

# Creating heatmap
Meco_16S_Func$plot_spe_func_perc(add_facet = TRUE,
                                 order_x = Order16S)+
    scale_fill_gradient2(low = "#FF0000", mid ="#CCFFFF", high = "#001A1A" ,midpoint = 0.00001, na.value = "#FF0000")+
    labs(title = "Heatmap of functional diversity (16S data)")


ggsave(paste(datafolder,"Functional_analysis_ASV/16S_Func_Heat.png"), device = png, width = 2000, height = 1200, units = "px", dpi = 144, limitsize = FALSE)
ggsave(paste(datafolder,"Functional_analysis_ASV/ASV_16S_Func_Heat2.png"), device = png, width = 6000, height = 1200, units = "px", dpi = 144, limitsize = FALSE)
#Note the one sample in the middle that is different is B077; Higher levels of chloroplasts, photoautotrophy and also  human associated: maybe Biofilm? Algae? Moss?

# Merged By topology
Meco_16S_Funcx  <- Meco_16S_Rare$merge_samples("topology")

Meco_16S_Funcx  <- trans_func$new(Meco_16S_Funcx)
Meco_16S_Funcx$cal_spe_func(prok_database = "FAPROTAX")
Func16Sx         <- as.data.frame(Meco_16S_Funcx$res_spe_func)

Meco_16S_Funcx$cal_spe_func_perc(abundance_weighted = TRUE, perc = FALSE, dec = 8)
Func16SxPerc     <- as.data.frame(Meco_16S_Funcx$res_spe_func_perc)

# creating order from chaos
Ord16STest      <- Meco_16S_Rare$clone()
Ord16STest      <- Ord16STest$merge_samples("topology")
Ord16STest$cal_betadiv(method = "jaccard")
Ord16SBeta      <- trans_beta$new(dataset = Ord16STest, measure = "jaccard")
Ord16SBeta$cal_ordination(method = "PCoA")
Scores          <- scores(Ord16SBeta$res_ordination, choices = c(1,2), display = "sites")
Order16SGroup   <- Ord16STest$sample_names()[order(radial_theta(Scores))]

rm(Ord16STest, Ord16SBeta, Scores)

# plot

Meco_16S_Funcx$plot_spe_func_perc(add_facet = TRUE,
                                 order_x = Order16SGroup) +
    scale_fill_gradient2(low = "#FF0000", mid = "#CCFFFF", high = "#001A1A",midpoint = 0.00001, na.value = "#FF0000")+
    labs(title = "Heatmap of functional diversity grouped by topology (16S data)")

write.csv(Func16SxPerc, paste(datafolder,"Functional_analysis_ASV/ASV_16S_Func_topology.csv")
ggsave(paste(datafolder,"Functional_analysis_ASV/ASV_16S_Func_topology.png"), device = png, width = 2000, height = 1200, units = "px", dpi = 144, limitsize = FALSE)

#Meco_16S_Rare$sample_table$group <- paste(Meco_16S_Rare$sample_table$city, #Meco_16S_Rare$sample_table$topology, sep = "_")

# Merged By group
Meco_16S_Funcx  <- Meco_16S_Rare$merge_samples("topology")

Meco_16S_Funcx  <- trans_func$new(Meco_16S_Funcx)
Meco_16S_Funcx$cal_spe_func(prok_database = "FAPROTAX")
Func16Sx         <- as.data.frame(Meco_16S_Funcx$res_spe_func)

Meco_16S_Funcx$cal_spe_func_perc(abundance_weighted = TRUE, perc = FALSE, dec = 8)
Func16SxPerc     <- as.data.frame(Meco_16S_Funcx$res_spe_func_perc)

# creating order from chaos
Ord16STest      <- Meco_16S_Rare$clone()
Ord16STest      <- Ord16STest$merge_samples("topology")
Ord16STest$cal_betadiv(method = "jaccard")
Ord16SBeta      <- trans_beta$new(dataset = Ord16STest, measure = "jaccard")
Ord16SBeta$cal_ordination(method = "PCoA")
Scores<- scores(Ord16SBeta$res_ordination, choices = c(1,2), display = "sites")
Order16SGroup   <- Ord16STest$sample_names()[order(radial_theta(Scores))]

rm(Ord16STest, Ord16SBeta, Scores)

library(viridis)
library(scales)
# plot
Meco_16S_Funcx$res_spe_func_perc_trans$value <- ifelse(Meco_16S_Funcx$res_spe_func_perc_trans$value==NA,0,Meco_16S_Funcx$res_spe_func_perc_trans$value)

Meco_16S_Funcx$plot_spe_func_perc(add_facet = TRUE,
                                 order_x = Order16SGroup) +
    scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "viridis", na.value = "white")+
    labs(title = "Heatmap of functional diversity grouped by city and topology (16S data)")
```
